maintainer_note: Keep synchronized with hf_bundle_scanner/hf_bundle_scanner/configlint.py (emitted rule_id values) and hf_bundle_scanner/hf_bundle_scanner/dispatch.py (which configlint rule_ids set config_risk / aggregate exit). Add new rows when configlint grows; cite primary standards (OWASP GenAI / NIST / vendor docs).

global_references.title: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications (project hub)
LLM Scanner — Threat model & taxonomy (phase0, in-repo)
NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF)
Hugging Face Hub — Pickle scanning (unsafe serialization class)

rules.owasp_note: OWASP GenAI list numbering differs from older slide decks; map to Supply chain / Insecure plugin or extension / Excessive agency themes — see genai.owasp.org LLM Top 10 for the canonical wording.
rules.vs_trust_remote_code: This IS the reference worst-case static loader signal in default CI: it can enable execution of Hub-supplied Python during load in typical stacks.
rules.legend_takeaway: Stop / waive with named controls; do not treat as a hyperparameter tweak.
rules.expert_guidance: Treat as security + legal + procurement: require explicit waiver, revision pinning, sandboxed CI materialization, and deny-by-default in prod unless tightly bounded. Pair with secrets rotation if any Hub token scopes are broad.

rules.owasp_note: Custom class mapping is a supply-chain / unexpected-code-path story adjacent to insecure integration patterns.
rules.vs_trust_remote_code: Different mechanism than trust_remote_code, but still a high static concern: unexpected class wiring can execute surprising code paths.
rules.legend_takeaway: Blocking in default CI; require provenance for each mapped class.
rules.expert_guidance: Assign named owners per class mapping, require code review + SBOM linkage, and document waivers. Do not merge silently.

rules.owasp_note: Primarily integrity / operational reliability; fix before interpreting other loader flags.
rules.vs_trust_remote_code: Not a remote-code narrative by itself — the file does not parse, which can hide misconfigurations and break reproducibility.
rules.legend_takeaway: Fix JSON first; blocking default CI is intentional.
rules.expert_guidance: Treat as release hygiene: repair JSON, re-run scan, then revisit loader posture. Prevents false negatives on downstream checks.

rules.owasp_note: Hygiene / reproducibility / integration complexity — not equivalent to remote code execution by itself.
rules.vs_trust_remote_code: Much lower incident class than trust_remote_code in typical stacks: does not assert arbitrary Hub Python will run; flags legacy fast-tokenizer paths that complicate audits.
rules.legend_takeaway: Engineering + audit follow-up; not an automatic emergency bridge.
rules.expert_guidance: Route to ML platform owners: confirm tokenizer provenance, add regression tests, pin revisions. Escalate to security only if regulated explainability or strict reproducibility policies apply.

rules.owasp_note: Sensitive information / secret handling — closer to disclosure and compliance than to loader RCE.
rules.vs_trust_remote_code: Different class: credential exposure and secret sprawl risk, not the same as execute Hub code at load.
rules.legend_takeaway: Secrets hygiene: rotate, remove from repos, use secret stores.
rules.expert_guidance: Treat as secret-management: rotate tokens, block merges that embed secrets, prefer OIDC/short-lived creds. Escalate if token scopes touch production data planes.

references.title: Hugging Face Hub — User access tokens

rules.owasp_note: Supply-chain / artifact integrity: explicit opt-out of safetensors hints pickle-era weight paths may be acceptable.
rules.vs_trust_remote_code: Not remote code at load; it is a **weights format / deserialization posture** signal that pairs with `.bin` risk in threat models.
rules.legend_takeaway: Align with org policy (safetensors-first); track as backlog unless policy makes it blocking.
rules.expert_guidance: If your org is safetensors-only on paper, reconcile config with weights on disk. Otherwise treat as ML engineering: prefer safetensors, document exceptions, do not confuse with trust_remote_code.

rules.owasp_note: Supply chain / integrity governance when policy encodes allowed formats and hashes.
rules.vs_trust_remote_code: Governance and integrity posture, not the same statement as trust_remote_code unless policy explicitly encodes that equivalence.
rules.legend_takeaway: Artifact/policy mismatch — waive with sign-off or remediate formats.
rules.expert_guidance: Leadership chooses waive-with-recorded-risk vs convert/drop artifacts. Pull in audit/procurement if external claims (e.g., safetensors-only) exist.
